1.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
1.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

1.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 900 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd. Race rhythm session: groove stroke quality early, then hold sustainable pacing. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 5×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. Sight every 6–8 strokes; practice breathing to both sides for chop or sun glare. Swim these repeats hands-only by default; paddles optional for a few repeats only if shoulders feel good—not the entire main set. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
1.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

1.name: Run Intervals — 4×1000m
1.description: Warm up 10 min easy. 4×1000m at 10K / tempo pace with 90 sec jog recovery between. Cool down 10 min. Focus on consistent splits, not all-out.

1.name: Swim Threshold — 1100 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
1.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

1.name: Long Ride — 2.0 hr
1.description: Aerobic endurance ride at Z2. Maintain 60–70 rpm cadence. Nutrition practice: eat every 40–45 minutes. No surges.

1.name: Long Run — 8.5 mi
1.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

2.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
2.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

2.name: Endurance Swim — 1300 yd
2.description: Warm up 400 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Fingertip Drag (high-elbow recovery). Main set — 2×300 yd easy aerobic with 30 sec rest between. Cool down 200 yd.

2.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
2.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

2.name: Run Intervals — 4×1000m

Cool down 200 yd.

11.name: Bike Threshold — 3×20 min

11.name: Swim Time Trial — 1400 yd
11.description: Warm up 500 yd with progressive build (last 100 at moderate effort). 400 yd MAX effort — sustained hard, hold form. 4 min easy recovery (back-and-forth jogs or float). 200 yd MAX effort — leave nothing. Cool down 300 yd easy. The engine will recompute your 100yd pace target from these two splits.

11.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
11.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

13.name: Bike VO2max — 3×5 min
13.description: Warm up 15 min. 3×5 min at 110–120% FTP (Zone 5) with 3 min easy recovery. Cool down 10 min. Short, maximal efforts — go hard.

13.name: Race-Pace Sustained Swim — 800 yd
13.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 3×600 yd at race pace (sustainable hard — what you can hold for the race-distance swim). 45 sec rest between. Cool down 300 yd easy.

13.name: Easy Ride — 2.0 hr
13.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

13.name: Half-Marathon Pace Run — 3 mi
13.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 3 mi at 70.3 / half-marathon run pace (Z3–Z4), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

13.name: Swim Threshold — 1300 yd
13.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Single-Arm Freestyle (rotation + balanced pull). 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

13.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
13.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

13.description: Brick bike at Zone 2. Build leg feel for the transition. Maintain steady power throughout.

13.name: Brick — Run 3.5 mi off the bike
13.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Easy Z2 throughout.

13.name: Half-Marathon Pace Run — 5 mi
13.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 5 mi at 70.3 / half-marathon run pace (Z3–Z4), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

14.name: Bike VO2max — 4×5 min
14.description: Warm up 15 min. 4×5 min at 110–120% FTP (Zone 5) with 3 min easy recovery. Cool down 10 min. Short, maximal efforts — go hard.

14.name: Open Water Skills — 2000 yd
14.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Open water if accessible — otherwise pool with sighting every 6 strokes throughout the main set. 4×100 yd at race-start hard effort (settle into race pace by the end of each 100), 30 sec rest. 6×200 yd at race-rhythm sustained effort with sighting every 6 strokes — bilateral breathing alternating sides. Cool down 200 yd easy. If you have access to a group, practice both lead position and drafting (feet/hip-side).

14.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
14.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

14.name: Tempo Run — 3 mi at threshold
14.description: Warm up 1.5 mi easy, then 3 mi at lactate threshold (comfortably hard — 7–8 RPE, can say a few words). Cool down 1.5 mi easy.

14.name: Swim Threshold — 2000 yd
14.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Sighting Drill (head-up race-day navigation). 8×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 4×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

14.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
14.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

14.name: Brick — Bike 2.8 hr
14.description: Race-simulation brick — ride 124 min at Zone 2 to build durability, then close with 41 min at expected race power (Z3, ~0.78-0.82 IF for 70.3 / 0.62-0.68 IF for full IM). Stay aero through the close. Transition quickly into the run.

14.name: Brick — Run 5 mi off the bike
14.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Target race pace last half.

14.name: Long Run — 12 mi
14.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

17.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 1300 yd
17.description: Warm up 300 yd. Race rhythm session: groove stroke quality early, then hold sustainable pacing. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 7×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. Sight every 6–8 strokes; practice breathing to both sides for chop or sun glare. Swim these repeats hands-only by default; paddles optional for a few repeats only if shoulders feel good—not the entire main set. Cool down 200 yd.

17.name: Easy Run — 4 mi
17.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

17.name: Race-Week Activation Swim — 800 yd
17.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 4×50 yd build accelerations (start easy, finish strong; long rest between — pure neuromuscular sharpener, NOT a hard interval). 2×100 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

17.name: Test 70.3 race
17.description: Race day. Swim 1.2mi → Bike 56mi → Run 13.1mi. No add-on training; execute pacing and fueling.

10: Week 10 long ride is shorter than the typical build floor (2.25h vs 2.5h). Lengthening toward 2.5h+ builds the bike-leg endurance the race demands.
13: Week 13 has no long run scheduled — race-specific plans target a 11+ mi long run for durability.

1.explanation: Three swims is a bump in load; the plan parked the extra day on Wednesday instead of Tuesday.
1.science_note: Swim fitness rises slowly; one fewer hard week rarely costs race swim, but it can protect bike and run.

primary_option.label: Keep the third swim on Wednesday

secondary_option.label: Park the third swim and stay at two swims

3.explanation: This week is a recovery week, so the planner kept the safer default instead of asking you to juggle hard sessions.
3.science_note: Recovery and taper weeks protect fitness you already built; big swaps add risk without much extra reward.

17.explanation: Race week keeps the plan tight, so we are not offering alternate days for this third swim change.
17.science_note: The days right around race day are for sharpness and rest, not for re-opening big training choices.
